content.name: knowledge_lookup
content.result: Only a handful operate commercially: Akademik Lomonosov (2x35 MWe) and HTR-PM (210 MWe); others in licensing.

steps.content: Small modular reactors (SMRs) — factory-built units under 300 MWe — remain mostly pre-commercial. Only Russia's floating Akademik Lomonosov and China's HTR-PM operate today; NuScale, GE-Hitachi's BWRX-300, Rolls-Royce and X-energy sit in licensing or early build. Economics are the hinge: first-of-a-kind costs are steep, and the 2023 NuScale/UAMPS cancellation showed the risk, yet surging data-center power demand is reviving order books. Proponents cite passive safety, siting flexibility, and load-following value. The next few years of first-of-a-kind construction will decide whether serialized factory production can deliver the promised cost curve.
